nuggets.headline: Pete Rango's career pivoted after winning a Miami producer competition with Parris Pierce
nuggets.text: Rango's main production partner has long been Parris Pierce. After moving to Florida, they competed in i-Standard’s Music Producers competition, winning in Miami. This victory connected them with the production duo The Renegades, which directly led to studio sessions with various artists and kickstarted Rango's industry work.

source.title: Meet Pete Rango - Voyage MIA Magazine | Miami City Guide
source.quoteSnippet: Parris has been one of my main production partners ever since. Parris and I competed in i-Standard’s Music Producers competition twice... We ended up winning the competition in Miami and had the chance to meet producer duo The Renegades.

nuggets.imageCaption: Producer and artist developer Pete Rango at work in the studio.

nuggets.headline: Pete Rango's musical path began after his family fled persecution in Colombia
nuggets.text: Born in Bogota, Colombia, Rango first became interested in music at age nine, teaching himself guitar. His family moved to Fairfax, Virginia, when he was 11 to escape persecution from guerrilla forces. It was in the U.S. that he joined the school band, playing clarinet and then percussion, setting a foundation for his genre-spanning career.

source.title: Meet Pete Rango - Voyage MIA Magazine | Miami City Guide
source.quoteSnippet: I was born in Bogota, Colombia... My family then moved to Fairfax, VA when I was 11 because we were being persecuted by the guerrilla forces. I then joined the school band. I played clarinet and then moved to snare/percussion.

nuggets.imageCaption: Oms at Play by Pete Rango

nuggets.headline: for a similar sonic finish, listen to Nia Sultana's work with Saki Engineering
nuggets.text: The final polish on Pete Rango's tracks comes from Saki Engineering, who also handles mixing and mastering for R&B artist Nia Sultana. You can hear that shared attention to detail and sonic depth on Sultana's project "Bigger Dreams." It’s a direct throughline from the same person behind the console.

source.title: Mixing & Mastering Engineers, Producers & Songwriters who ...
source.quoteSnippet: Credits: Pete Rango, Black Youngsta, Nia Sultana

nuggets.imageCaption: R&B artist Nia Sultana, whose music is also mixed by Pete Rango's engineer.

artistSummary: Pete Rango is a Colombian-born producer and creative director whose work spans hip-hop, electronic, and R&B. His career is defined by artist development and a path that took him from a high school metal band to engineering for artists like XXXTentacion and landing placements on HBO.
